items.prompt: Solve `x + 7 = 19` and show how each step preserves equality.
items.expected_evidence: rewrite and justify bounded trigonometric expressions using core identities and equivalent forms
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates rewrite and justify bounded trigonometric expressions using core identities and equivalent forms with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Solve `4x = 28`, then check the solution by substitution.
items.expected_evidence: solve bounded trigonometric equations and explain the role of periodicity in the solution set
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates solve bounded trigonometric equations and explain the role of periodicity in the solution set with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Match the equation `n - 5 = 12` to a balance or missing-value situation and solve it.
items.expected_evidence: interpret transformed sine, cosine, and tangent graphs using analytic and graphical features together
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates interpret transformed sine, cosine, and tangent graphs using analytic and graphical features together with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why subtracting the same value from both sides of an equation preserves the solution set.
items.expected_evidence: rewrite and justify bounded trigonometric expressions using core identities and equivalent forms
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to rewrite and justify bounded trigonometric expressions using core identities and equivalent forms.

items.prompt: Explain what the variable represents and why inverse operations help isolate it.
items.expected_evidence: solve bounded trigonometric equations and explain the role of periodicity in the solution set
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to solve bounded trigonometric equations and explain the role of periodicity in the solution set.

items.prompt: A student solves `x + 5 = 13` by writing `x = 13 + 5`. Diagnose the first structural mistake. Focus on angle coordinate period fragmentation.
items.expected_evidence: rewrite and justify bounded trigonometric expressions using core identities and equivalent forms
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward rewrite and justify bounded trigonometric expressions using core identities and equivalent forms.

items.prompt: A student solves `3x = 18` by dividing only the `x`. Explain what went wrong. Focus on unit circle period opacity.
items.expected_evidence: solve bounded trigonometric equations and explain the role of periodicity in the solution set
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward solve bounded trigonometric equations and explain the role of periodicity in the solution set.

items.prompt: A gym charges a $12 signup fee plus $5 per class. Write and solve an equation for the number of classes if the total was $32.
items.expected_evidence: rewrite and justify bounded trigonometric expressions using core identities and equivalent forms
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves rewrite and justify bounded trigonometric expressions using core identities and equivalent forms in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A balance model has three equal bags and 6 loose counters on one side and 24 counters on the other. Write and solve the equation.
items.expected_evidence: solve bounded trigonometric equations and explain the role of periodicity in the solution set
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves solve bounded trigonometric equations and explain the role of periodicity in the solution set in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Two weeks later, solve `m/3 = 8` and explain how you know the result is reasonable.
items.expected_evidence: rewrite and justify bounded trigonometric expressions using core identities and equivalent forms
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates rewrite and justify bounded trigonometric expressions using core identities and equivalent forms on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Before solving trigonometric identities equations and analytic trig, complete a prerequisite check on trigonometric functions and unit circle and explain how it supports the current equation work.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether trigonometric functions and unit circle is secure enough to support trigonometric identities equations and analytic trig
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in trigonometric functions and unit circle from a current-node issue in trigonometric identities equations and analytic trig.

items.prompt: Use a quick distributive reasoning and combining like terms check to explain whether the learner is blocked by prerequisite structure or by trigonometric identities equations and analytic trig itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether distributive reasoning and combining like terms is secure enough to support trigonometric identities equations and analytic trig
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in distributive reasoning and combining like terms from a current-node issue in trigonometric identities equations and analytic trig.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: distributive reasoning and combining like terms
